headline: Site Health · technical issues cleared
standfirst: From the Semrush Site Audit crawler, captured June 5, 2026. Site Health Score climbed to 90/100 (top-tier band) — Errors dropped −33 and Warnings −20 since the last audit cycle. The technical foundation from Tasks 1–7 plus the duplicate-content and blocked-pages fixes (Tasks 15 + 19) drove the lift. AI Search Health hit 87/100 directly from the June 4 Task 18 schema deploy. The 75 audited pages still flag some unfinished work — meta descriptions on 43 pages, duplicate H1/title pairs on 10 — but the trajectory is now firmly in the green.
summary_lead: Site Health 90/100. Errors down −33 since the last crawl. Warnings down −20. AI Search Health 87/100 immediately reflects the Task 18 FAQPage + Yoast enrichment + Speakable deploy on June 4. Markup score 100/100. Site Performance 96/100. Internal Linking 95/100 (+3). The two remaining error categories and the meta-description gap are the small-batch cleanup queue for next month.

win_tiles.label: Site Health Score
win_tiles.tip: Semrush's overall technical health score weighted across crawlability, on-page, internal linking, performance, HTTPS, and markup. 90+ is top-tier band. Lifted from 85 → 90 this cycle by the Task 1–7 foundation plus the blocked-pages fix.

win_tiles.label: AI Search Health
win_tiles.tip: Semrush's measure of how well the site is set up to be cited by AI search engines (ChatGPT, Perplexity, Gemini, Google AI Overviews). Lifted from baseline directly by Task 18 — FAQPage schema, Yoast Organization enrichment, SpeakableSpecification.

win_tiles.label: Errors cleared
win_tiles.tip: Real errors flagged by Semrush dropped 33 instances since the last audit cycle. Only 2 errors remain in the current crawl.

win_tiles.label: Warnings cleared
win_tiles.tip: Warning-tier issues dropped 20 instances since the last audit. 131 remaining — most are informational notices (no canonical on multilingual pages, image lazy-loading hints) rather than errors.

win_tiles.label: Markup score
win_tiles.tip: Semrush detected zero markup errors across the crawled pages. Yoast schema + the Task 18 JSON-LD blocks both validate cleanly.

win_tiles.label: Internal Linking lift
win_tiles.tip: Internal linking score climbed 3 points to 95/100 after the broken-link fixes (Task 4) and the new SEO articles (Tasks 16, 17) added contextual internal links.

top_issues.note: Task 2 continued cleanup — most are low-traffic blog tag archives

top_issues.issue: Duplicate content in H1 and title
top_issues.note: Rewrite either H1 or title to differentiate intent
Trim to under 60 chars to avoid SERP truncation
Add FAQ schema, semantic markup — pattern from Task 18 reusable

top_issues.issue: Links with no anchor text (AI Search)

blocked_bots.note: Investigate — Google bot should not be blocked

rows.note: long tail of blog archive pages

qa_checklist.title: QA · regression-check on the engagement deploys (Task 25)
qa_checklist.subtitle: Automated checks that confirm previous wins didn't silently break

Yoast Org graph has CMA licence 20200000223

prose.title: What the audit shows.
paragraphs: Site Health 90, AI Search Health 87. Both are inside the top-tier band. The 5-point Site Health lift came from clearing 33 errors and 20 warnings — the cumulative effect of Tasks 1–7 (title tags, meta, H1s, internal/external link fixes, ALT, robots) plus Task 15 (duplicate content) and Task 19 (blocked pages). The 87 on AI Search Health is the direct result of June 4's Task 18 deploy: FAQPage schema, Yoast Organization enrichment, SpeakableSpecification.

Two error categories remain. Just 2 hard errors across the 100 crawled pages — both low-impact issues queued for the next monthly cycle. Warning count is 131, but the substance is concentrated in three categories: 43 pages with missing meta descriptions (mostly low-traffic blog tag archives — finishing Task 2 will clean this), 10 pages with duplicate H1 and title (rewrite one to differentiate), and 19 pages flagged as 'content not optimized for AI Search' (these are eligible for the same FAQ-schema pattern Task 18 used).

Core Web Vitals still 0/100 — Task 8 hasn't started. The CWV audit (5h, Month 1) is the blocker for measuring the LCP/CLS/INP numbers. Once it ships, the score will lift from zero baseline to whatever the actual measured number is. Task 8 also unblocks remediation work (image dimensions, JS deferral) which would lift Site Performance from 96 → likely 98+.

Internal Linking +3 this cycle. Score climbed from 92 to 95. The new SEO articles (Tasks 16 + 17) added contextual internal links across the product pages, and the broken-link fixes (Task 4) stopped equity from leaking into dead ends.

pull_quote.text: Site Health 90, AI Search Health 87. Foundation work shows up in the audit before it shows up in rankings.
